customer_anxiety_profile: Elena is on disability and fixed income (Social Security covers only her mortgage). She has real financial anxiety about any new payment obligation — not skepticism about solar in principle, but genuine inability to take on additional costs without her daughter's involvement. She is not hostile; she is constrained and needs the daughter as a co-participant before any commitment is possible.
unresolved_concerns: Credit score unknown — Elena believes it is 'six and something' but has not checked recently; daughter may need to assist with Credit Karma check
Payment structure not fully understood — Elena heard 'you still pay for the solar panels' and may still believe solar adds a new bill rather than replacing ComEd
No appointment booked — callback is unstructured with no locked time
sensitive_topics_raised: Disability / not working — Elena disclosed this twice with escalating emphasis
Fixed income — Social Security covers only mortgage; daughter sometimes pays electric bill
Prior solar pitch rejected — a previous rep offered a rental/lease arrangement Elena could not afford; she may conflate Ion's program with that experience
Daughter as financial co-participant — pays electric bill when Elena cannot; Elena named her as the person who will check the credit score
exact_promises_rep_made: Rep said she would send a text with a Credit Karma link for Elena to check her credit score for free
Rep confirmed the program would make electric bills lower than current ComEd payments
Rep stated 'guaranteed savings — we have to make it so it saves you money, or we can't even recommend it' — this is an ungrounded commitment the closer should be aware of
closer_should_know: The daughter is the de facto decision-maker and financial gatekeeper — Elena named her three times and the callback is contingent on the daughter checking the credit score. Do not attempt to advance this lead without the daughter on the call. Lead with empathy on the fixed-income situation before any payment framing, and clarify that solar replaces the ComEd bill rather than adding to it — Elena left the call uncertain on this point.
